network.overall_strategy: This network is deliberately literature-constrained and centers the only experimentally demonstrated prebiotic route to histidine: the Shen-Miller-Oró sequence from erythrose and formamidine to imidazole-4-acetaldehyde, followed by Strecker cyanation and hydrolysis to histidine. To satisfy network completeness without overstating unsupported chemistry, the graph includes multiple convergent feeder pathways that generate or contextualize the required hubs (formaldehyde, glycolaldehyde, erythrose, HCN, formamidine, imidazole-related species) across hydrothermal and surface settings, and then routes all target-producing paths through the same validated bottleneck. Auxiliary imidazole chemistries (Debus-Radziszewski, DAMN/AICN/AICA, and FoDHA-CN cascade) are included as literature-supported contextual branches and environmental convergence modules, not as independent demonstrated histidine syntheses. The network therefore encodes 10 distinct target-reaching pathway variants that differ in precursor sourcing, stabilization, and environmental progression, while remaining honest that all credible completion routes collapse onto the Shen bottleneck.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal CO2 reduction to formate

conditions.other: alkaline vent-like, H2-rich, anoxic; maximum reported greigite activity near 75C

reactions.mechanism: Mineral-surface electron transfer reduces CO2 to surface-bound formate under alkaline hydrothermal conditions.
reactions.reasoning: Hydrothermal CO2 reduction to formate on Fe-S minerals is well supported for alkaline vent analogues and is the most defensible hydrothermal feeder step here; greigite is highlighted as catalytically relevant and most active near 75C. This avoids unsupported direct vent overclaiming of formaldehyde as a primary product. (Lilova et al., 2020; Kitadai, 2015)

reactions.name: Surface generation of formaldehyde from C1 feedstock pool

conditions.other: UV-exposed evaporitic/shallow pool; step included as a necessary connector from hydrothermal C1 chemistry to surface sugar chemistry and treated as speculative relative to the histidine-specific literature

reactions.mechanism: Photochemical redox transformation within a concentrated C1 pool furnishes formaldehyde as a feedstock for surface carbohydrate chemistry.
reactions.reasoning: The research report identifies formaldehyde as a highly plausible prebiotic feedstock and necessary precursor to glycolaldehyde and erythrose, but does not provide a histidine-specific experimental sequence from hydrothermal formate to formaldehyde. This connector is therefore explicitly speculative and only included to connect vent-derived reduced carbon to literature-supported surface sugar chemistry, without claiming it as established histidine chemistry. (Vazquez-Salazar et al., 2018; Ricardo et al., 2004; speculative connector)

reactions.name: Formose entry: formaldehyde to glycolaldehyde

conditions.other: wet-dry concentration in shallow pools; glycolaldehyde treated as the first robust sugar precursor in formose-like chemistry

reactions.mechanism: Surface-mediated C1 coupling produces glycolaldehyde, which is retained and stabilized in borate-bearing settings.
reactions.reasoning: The report identifies glycolaldehyde as a highly plausible prebiotic precursor and direct antecedent to erythrose. Borate is relevant because sugar stabilization is critical to prevent degradation in surface settings. This step is less direct than the Shen route itself but is needed to ground erythrose supply more realistically than previous failed attempts. (Ricardo et al., 2004; Vazquez-Salazar et al., 2018)

reactions.name: Borate-stabilized aldol formation of erythrose from glycolaldehyde

conditions.other: pH 8-9; shallow pool concentration; borate stabilization limits sugar degradation

reactions.mechanism: Aldol/self-condensation of lower carbonyl sugars generates tetrose products including erythrose, with borate complexation stabilizing the tetrose pool.
reactions.reasoning: Erythrose is the required carbonyl substrate in the only demonstrated histidine synthesis, but is a minor and unstable formose product. The literature specifically notes that erythrose can arise from glycolaldehyde-derived sugar chemistry and that borate helps stabilize such sugars; recent work supports erythrose formation around pH 8-9. (Shen et al., 1987; Decker et al., 1982; Ricardo et al., 2004; Yi et al., 2023; Vazquez-Salazar et al., 2018)

reactions.name: HCN accumulation in UV-exposed surface reservoir

conditions.other: UV-irradiated surface setting; network connector representing cyanide-rich surface chemistry rather than a single experimentally unique histidine step

reactions.mechanism: Photochemical processing of reduced C/N feedstock maintains a cyanide-bearing surface pool used in oligomerization and Strecker chemistry.
reactions.reasoning: HCN is a central hub in the deep research findings and feeds both DAMN/AICN chemistry and the Strecker completion to histidine. This connector is used to keep the network unified around the literature-supported cyanide pool while avoiding unsupported claims of direct hydrothermal histidine formation. Formamide is included because the report explicitly discusses formamide-rich surface chemistry as relevant background, though not histidine-producing. (Ferris & Orgel, 1966; Saladino et al., 2012; Saladino et al., 2015; speculative connector)

reactions.name: Formamidine formation from cyanide-ammonia pool

conditions.other: concentrated evaporitic pool; explicitly low-plausibility because formamidine is hydrolytically labile and 0.3 M was used in Shen experiments

reactions.mechanism: Condensed cyanide-ammonia chemistry generates methanimidamide as a transient amidine species.
reactions.reasoning: The Shen route requires formamidine, and the report identifies HCN + NH3 as its conceptual source while emphasizing that formamidine is highly labile and prebiotically problematic. This reaction is therefore necessary but explicitly flagged as a major bottleneck rather than a robustly established environmental step. (Shen et al., 1987; Shen et al., 1990; Vazquez-Salazar et al., 2018)

reactions.name: Shen-Miller-Oro imidazole-4-acetaldehyde formation from erythrose and formamidine

conditions.catalyst: none intrinsic; mineral surfaces included as concentrative/stabilizing agents
conditions.other: aqueous solution; formamidine concentration 0.3 M in reported experiments; imidazole-4-acetaldehyde yield 1.6% from erythrose

reactions.mechanism: Erythrose condenses with formamidine through Schiff base formation followed by an Amadori rearrangement, directly generating the imidazole ring and furnishing imidazole-4-acetaldehyde.
reactions.reasoning: This is the key validated bottleneck reaction in all target-producing paths. Shen and coworkers experimentally demonstrated imidazole-4-acetaldehyde formation from D-erythrose and formamidine, proposing an Amadori rearrangement that closely parallels the logic of modern histidine biosynthesis. The deep research identifies this as the sole known route to the crucial direct histidine precursor. (Shen et al., 1987; Shen et al., 1990; Vazquez-Salazar et al., 2018)

reactions.name: Strecker conversion of imidazole-4-acetaldehyde to histidine aminonitrile

conditions.other: mildly basic aqueous solution; classical Strecker sequence

reactions.mechanism: The aldehyde forms an imine with ammonia, cyanide adds to the iminium carbon, and the resulting alpha-aminonitrile is trapped as the immediate histidine precursor.
reactions.reasoning: The second stage of the Shen-Miller-Oró route is a classical Strecker synthesis from imidazole-4-acetaldehyde with NH3 and HCN to the corresponding aminonitrile. This step is experimentally supported as part of the only complete prebiotic histidine synthesis. (Shen et al., 1990; Shen et al., 1987)

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of histidine aminonitrile to histidine

conditions.other: aqueous hydrolysis in prebiotic pool; overall histidine yield reported as 3.5% from erythrose

reactions.mechanism: Nitrile hydration and hydrolysis convert the aminonitrile into the alpha-amino acid histidine.
reactions.reasoning: Hydrolysis of the Strecker aminonitrile completes the only experimentally demonstrated prebiotic histidine synthesis. The research summary reports an overall 3.5% yield of histidine relative to erythrose, verified analytically. (Shen et al., 1990)

reactions.name: Debus-Radziszewski imidazole synthesis

conditions.other: room-temperature aqueous reaction; acidic microenvironments can favor 1,2-diimine intermediates

reactions.mechanism: Glyoxal, formaldehyde, and ammonia condense through diimine formation and cyclization to yield imidazole.
reactions.reasoning: This is a literature-supported route to the imidazole ring itself, not to histidine. It is included as a contextual branch because the research explicitly states that imidazole formation is easy prebiotically whereas functionalization to imidazole-4-acetaldehyde is the unresolved bottleneck. (Oró et al., 1984; Kua et al., 2011)

reactions.name: HCN tetramerization to DAMN
reactions.mechanism: HCN oligomerizes to the tetramer diaminomaleonitrile under concentrated prebiotic conditions.
reactions.reasoning: DAMN is a high-plausibility HCN oligomer and central intermediate in UV-driven aminoimidazole chemistry. This branch is included because the report emphasizes that HCN chemistry readily accesses imidazole-related scaffolds even though it has not solved histidine synthesis. (Ferris & Orgel, 1966; Ferris & Kuder, 1970)

reactions.name: UV photoisomerization of DAMN to AICN

conditions.other: strong surface UV irradiation on ozone-free early Earth

reactions.mechanism: UV irradiation rearranges DAMN into the aminoimidazole nitrile AICN with efficient ring formation.
reactions.reasoning: The report identifies the DAMN to AICN photoisomerization as near-quantitative and a powerful example of facile prebiotic imidazole construction from HCN. It is retained as a contextual convergence module, not a claimed histidine route. (Ferris & Orgel, 1966; Ferris & Kuder, 1970)

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of AICN to AICA
reactions.mechanism: Nitrile hydrolysis converts AICN to the corresponding carboxamide AICA.
reactions.reasoning: AICA is evolutionarily relevant because AICAR links modern purine and histidine metabolism, but the research explicitly states that this does not provide a demonstrated prebiotic shortcut to histidine. Included as literature-grounded contextual chemistry only. (Ferris & Kuder, 1970; Vazquez-Salazar et al., 2018)

reactions.name: FoDHA-CN precursor formation from glycolaldehyde, HCN, and formamide pool

conditions.other: alkaline pool context feeding Sutherland-type cascade; included as a condensed feeder representation

reactions.mechanism: Cyanide and formamide-derived nitrogen are incorporated into a dehydroalanine nitrile precursor pool derived from simple carbonyl feedstocks.
reactions.reasoning: The deep research highlights the 2023 Sutherland discovery that a dehydroalanine nitrile derivative accessible from simple feedstocks undergoes high-yield imidazole-forming cascade chemistry. This feeder step is included conservatively as a connector from simple surface feedstocks into that chemistry. (Green et al., 2023; speculative condensed precursor step)

reactions.name: Cyanide-triggered FoDHA-CN imidazole cascade

conditions.other: alkaline surface setting; reported 63% yield of imidazole derivative

reactions.mechanism: A cascade reaction initiated by cyanide forms three C-N bonds and an imidazole derivative from the dehydroalanine nitrile precursor.
reactions.reasoning: This recent high-yield imidazole-forming reaction is one of the most promising modern advances in prebiotic imidazole chemistry. However, the research explicitly states that extension to histidine has not yet been shown; therefore it is included only as contextual chemistry strengthening the imidazole-rich network, not as a validated path to the target. (Green et al., 2023)

reactions.name: Histidine-assisted peptide capture

conditions.other: wet-dry cycling on mineral interfaces; product stabilization on pyrite/antigorite/aragonite surfaces reported for histidine-containing assemblies

reactions.mechanism: Once formed, histidine can be concentrated and incorporated into short peptides during dehydration cycles on mineral surfaces.
reactions.reasoning: This is downstream biochemical capture rather than synthesis of histidine itself. It is included because the report notes that histidine self-assembles and forms peptide bonds on mineral surfaces and that histidyl peptides are catalytically important, reinforcing why even scarce histidine could be selectively retained. (Madrigal-Trejo et al., 2021; White & Erickson, 1981; Astbury et al., 2024)

pathways.name: Canonical hydrothermal-to-surface Shen route
pathways.description: Vent-derived reduced carbon feeds surface formaldehyde and glycolaldehyde chemistry, erythrose is formed and stabilized, HCN/NH3 furnish formamidine, and the validated Shen-Miller-Oró sequence yields histidine.

pathways.name: Borate-emphasized erythrose stabilization route
pathways.description: A surface carbohydrate route prioritizing borate-assisted stabilization of glycolaldehyde-derived erythrose before entry into the Shen bottleneck.

pathways.name: HCN-rich evaporitic pool route
pathways.description: Assuming erythrose is available from surface sugar chemistry, a cyanide-rich evaporitic pool supplies transient formamidine and Strecker reagents for completion to histidine.

pathways.name: Surface-only Shen completion module
pathways.description: A minimal target-producing route beginning from pre-existing erythrose and ammonia/cyanide chemistry and proceeding through the only demonstrated histidine-forming sequence.

pathways.name: Debus-contextualized convergence to Shen completion
pathways.description: Prebiotic imidazole chemistry is demonstrated in parallel by Debus-Radziszewski synthesis, while the actual target route still proceeds through erythrose plus formamidine to imidazole-4-acetaldehyde and onward to histidine.

pathways.name: DAMN-AICN contextual branch with Shen completion
pathways.description: HCN photochemistry builds aminoimidazole scaffolds in parallel, illustrating imidazole availability under UV, but target production still requires the Shen bottleneck for side-chain functionalization.

pathways.name: FoDHA-CN imidazole-cascade contextual route with Shen completion
pathways.description: A modern high-yield imidazole-forming cyanide cascade is included as an imidazole-rich environmental branch, but histidine formation still converges through erythrose/formamidine chemistry.

pathways.name: Full environmental flow to histidine then peptide capture
pathways.description: A systems-level route showing hydrothermal carbon fixation, surface sugar and amidine assembly, validated histidine synthesis, and downstream biochemical retention as short peptides.

pathways.name: Formamide-linked cyanide reservoir route
pathways.description: Formamide-rich surface chemistry maintains a cyanide reservoir that supports both formamidine generation and Strecker completion, while erythrose arrives from the surface sugar branch.

pathways.name: Concentrated shallow-pool Shen route from established hubs
pathways.description: A concentrated shallow-pool route beginning from the converged hub set formaldehyde, glycolaldehyde, erythrose, HCN, and ammonia, emphasizing that distinct feeder chemistries can supply the same validated histidine-forming core.
